actionUnits.condition: Scene object has BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody; allowed action is Trigger. Attached MonoBehaviour matches Fire.cs (script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) exactly per provided scene serialization (fields: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo set). No UnityEvent/m_Calls are present on this component, and Fire exposes no public parameterless methods (public methods are NotifyNeighborState(bool), UpdateFireState(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), SetIsOn(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[], int)). Therefore, there are no callable no-arg methods for a manual Trigger. To fully test behavior, we need either: (a) a UnityEvent/EventTrigger wired to call a parameterless proxy, (b) an external water system that invokes IWaterInteractable.WaterHit on collision, or (c) neighbor Fire instances configured so their state changes call NotifyNeighborState on this instance.
